vocabulary.definition: to make a low sound of complaint or dismay
vocabulary.example: Still, first year Gryffindors only had Potions with the Slytherins, so they didn’t have to put up with Malfoy much. Or at least, they didn’t until they spotted a notice pinned up in the Gryffindor common room that made them all groan.

vocabulary.definition: a magical stick used for flying
vocabulary.example: “Typical,” said Harry darkly. “Just what I always wanted. To make a fool of myself on a broomstick in front of Malfoy.”
Neville was trying to remember what he’d forgotten when Draco Malfoy, who was passing the Gryffindor table, snatched the Remembrall out of his hand.

vocabulary.definition: to fly high in the air
vocabulary.example: Harry ignored her. Blood was pounding in his ears. He mounted the broom and kicked hard against the ground and up, up he soared; air rushed through his hair, and his robes whipped out behind him and in a rush of fierce joy he realized he’d found something he could do without being taught—this was easy, this was wonderful.

vocabulary.definition: a long pointed spear thrown in sport
vocabulary.example: He leaned forward and grasped the broom tightly in both hands, and it shot toward Malfay like a javelin.

He leaned forward and pointed his broom handle down—next second he was gathering speed in a steep dive, racing the ball—wind whistled in his ears, mingled with the screams of people watching—he stretched out his hand—a foot from the ground he caught it, just in time to pull his broom straight, and he toppled gently onto the grass with the Remembrall clutched safely in his fist.

vocabulary.definition: the Quidditch player who catches the Golden Snitch
vocabulary.example: “Potter, this is Oliver Wood. Wood—I’ve found you a Seeker.”

vocabulary.definition: a formal fight between two people using magic or weapons
vocabulary.example: “I’d take you on anytime on my own,” said Malfoy. “Tonight, if you want. Wizard’s duel. Wands only—no contact. What’s the matter? Never heard of a wizard’s duel before, I suppose?”

vocabulary.definition: a loose robe worn before or after bathing or bed
vocabulary.example: A lamp flickered on. It was Hermione Granger, wearing a pink bathrobe and a frown.

vocabulary.definition: a hidden door in a floor or ceiling
vocabulary.example: “No, not the floor. It was standing on a trapdoor. It’s obviously guarding something.”
They were looking straight into the eyes of a monstrous dog, a dog that filled the whole space between ceiling and floor.

vocabulary.definition: to make someone so frightened they cannot move
vocabulary.example: “This way!” Harry mouthed to the others and, petrified, they began to creep down a long gallery full of suits of armor.

characters.name: Harry Potter
characters.description: Discovers a talent for flying and is picked as Gryffindor Seeker.

characters.name: Draco Malfoy
characters.description: Bullies Neville, challenges Harry to a midnight duel, and may have set a trap.

characters.name: Professor McGonagall
characters.description: Catches Harry flying but recruits him for the Quidditch team instead of expelling him.

characters.name: Hermione Granger
characters.description: Warns Harry about breaking rules, then gets locked out and joins the midnight adventure.

characters.name: Ron Weasley
characters.description: Acts as Harry’s second for the duel and runs with him through the castle at night.

background.hook: Flying lessons force Gryffindors and Slytherins together. Harry breaks the rules to chase Malfoy on a broomstick—and lands on the Quidditch team. That night, a wizard’s duel leads the friends straight into deadly trouble.
background.readingFocus: Follow Harry’s first flight, McGonagall’s surprising reaction, and what the three-headed dog in the forbidden corridor is guarding.

miniCheck.stem: A broomstick in this story is used to —

options.text: a formal magical fight between two people

comprehension.stem: What is this chapter mainly about?

options.text: Harry’s flying lesson, becoming Seeker, a midnight duel trap, and discovering the three-headed dog
Harry’s first Potions lesson with Snape
Harry shopping for a wand in Diagon Alley

comprehension.explanation: The chapter covers flying, Quidditch recruitment, the failed duel, and the forbidden corridor.

options.text: A glass ball that glows red if you have forgotten something
A spell to fix broken bones

comprehension.explanation: Neville’s grandmother sends him a Remembrall that turns scarlet when he has forgotten something.

comprehension.stem: What happens to Neville during the flying lesson?

options.text: He shoots into the air early, falls, and breaks his wrist
He is the first to make his broom jump to his hand
He refuses to touch a broomstick

comprehension.explanation: Neville pushes off before the whistle and falls from a great height.

comprehension.stem: What position does McGonagall recruit Harry for?

options.text: Seeker on the Gryffindor Quidditch team

comprehension.explanation: She tells Oliver Wood she has found him a Seeker.

comprehension.stem: How does Harry feel when McGonagall marches him away after he flies?

options.text: Sure he is about to be expelled
Proud and certain he will be praised

comprehension.explanation: Harry thinks he hasn’t even lasted two weeks and will be packing his bags.

comprehension.stem: Why does Hermione think Malfoy never meant to duel Harry?

options.text: Malfoy must have tipped off Filch that someone would be in the trophy room
Malfoy was afraid of Ron’s second

comprehension.explanation: Hermione says Filch knew someone would be there, so Malfoy likely warned him.

comprehension.stem: What is guarding the trapdoor in the forbidden corridor?
comprehension.explanation: They stare into the eyes of a monstrous dog with three heads standing on a trapdoor.

vocabulary.stem: In Quidditch, a 'Seeker' is the player who —

options.text: chases and catches the Golden Snitch
serves dinner in the Great Hall

vocabulary.explanation: McGonagall tells Wood she has found him a Seeker.

vocabulary.stem: He mounted the broom and kicked hard against the ground and up, up he ___.
vocabulary.explanation: Harry soars into the air when he chases Malfoy.

options.text: a hidden door in a floor
a glass ball that glows red

vocabulary.explanation: Hermione notices the three-headed dog is standing on a trapdoor.

vocabulary.stem: Which word is closest in meaning to 'monstrous'?
vocabulary.explanation: The dog in the corridor is described as monstrous.

vocabulary.stem: It was Hermione Granger, wearing a pink ___ and a frown.
vocabulary.explanation: Hermione waits in the common room in her pink bathrobe.

cloze.title: The Midnight Duel
paragraphs: When Gryffindor and Slytherin shared flying lessons, Ron and Harry (1) ___ at the notice. Malfoy later (2) ___ Neville’s Remembrall, and Harry chased him on a (3) ___.
Harry (4) ___ into the air and made a daring (5) ___ to catch the glass ball. McGonagall made him Gryffindor (6) ___ instead of expelling him.
That night a wizard’s (7) ___ lured them out of bed. They fled Filch and found a monstrous dog guarding a trapdoor.

gaps.explanation: The first years groan when they see the flying lesson notice.
Malfoy snatches Neville’s Remembrall at breakfast.
Harry mounts his broomstick to chase Malfoy.
Harry soars upward when he kicks off from the ground.
Harry makes a steep dive to catch the Remembrall.
McGonagall tells Wood she has found a Seeker.
Malfoy challenges Harry to a wizard’s duel at midnight.
